rationale: Traced the fix end-to-end. resolvePanelGateFacts swaps exec.RevParseRef for nonBeadTargetRevParse only when beadID=="", which ignores ResolveGateFacts's doomed "bead/"+beadID ref arg and rev-parses reg.Panel.Target instead, so MissingRef/GitErr/HeadSHA are now honestly populated for non-bead panels, un-shadowing legs 6/8/9/10. TestPanelVerify_NonBeadStaleness (advanced target) and TestPanelTally_NonBeadStaleBlocks prove no PASS, BLOCK present, tally exits non-zero, no bead/ or mindspec-complete leak. The REJECT-at-un-advanced-target subtest + TestPanelTally_NonBeadRejectBlocks prove the same for leg 9. Leg 8 (incomplete) is proven via TestSanitizeNonBeadDecision using the REAL panel.PanelGateDecision (not hardcoded) confirming Action==Block survives sanitize untouched. panelTallyCmd's RunE correctly branches IsBead() to route non-bead through tallyExitActionNonBead (never tallyExitAction's <bead> literal). All ran green (go test ./cmd/mindspec ./internal/panel ./internal/complete). Git status clean, pinned at b641a9fe, file scope exactly the 3 listed files.
findings: Minor, non-blocking: legs 1/2 (staleness-advanced) and REJECT are proven with a full CLI-level test (runPanelVerbCmd against panelVerifyCmd/panelTallyCmd), but leg 8 (incomplete, < expected_reviewers) is only proven at the sanitizeNonBeadDecision unit level using directly-constructed panel.GateFacts, not via an E2E fixture where nonBeadPanelFixture seeds fewer verdict files than ExpectedReviewers. Functionally this is low-risk: nonBeadTargetRevParse and the CLI dispatch only branch on d.Action generically (already E2E-proven via the REJECT case, which reaches the same post-staleness code path with a matching SHA), and leg 8's Action is untouched by this bead's diff (internal/panel is zero-byte). Still, an explicit CLI-level incomplete-verdicts test would close the loop completely and is a reasonable follow-up, not a blocker for this bead.

No leg was found that could leak `references branch bead/` or `mindspec complete <bead>` for a non-bead panel — sanitizeNonBeadDecision strips the RawMergeFence("") suffix on legs 2/4/6/8/9/10, rewrites the leg-5 missing-ref sentence wholesale, and renames the leg-5b transient-error fragments; tallyExitActionNonBead's own Block recovery names a `panel create ... --round <N+1>` re-panel command, never `mindspec complete`.

panel verify always exits 0 (confirmed in code: RunE always `return nil` regardless of Action) — consistent with the spec's read-only contract; the falsification correctly checks the rendered text (PASS/BLOCK) rather than exit code for verify, and exit code for tally.
